key_insight: WEST is an open-source, plane-wave code that unifies multiple excited-state methods in a single framework and scales to very large materials simulations without explicitly computing virtual states.
engineering_relevance: It enables practical, GPU-accelerated prediction of quasiparticle, optical, and non-adiabatic properties for systems with more than a thousand atoms, supporting materials discovery workflows.
method: Full-frequency GW, Bethe-Salpeter equation, TDDFT, and quantum defect embedding in one codebase
Avoids explicit virtual electronic states using density functional and density matrix perturbation theory
Uses low-rank screening/exact-exchange representations, localization, and hierarchical parallelization with GPU acceleration
evidence_signal: The abstract reports near-ideal strong scaling to thousands of GPUs and representative applications across spin defects, perovskites, water, and ice.
watch_item: Performance and accuracy likely depend on careful workflow setup, parallel hardware availability, and method-specific convergence choices for large excited-state calculations.
paper_title: The WEST code for large-scale excited-state materials simulations
